message:
fix: handle codex_exec originator sessions correctly in Codex→Pi conversion

The converter was writing 0 bytes for message content at idx 4 when
the session had originator:codex_exec in session_meta.

Root cause: Codex sessions with originator:codex_exec produce events like
function_call and function_call_output that become canonical messages with
empty content but non-empty tool_calls/tool_results. The Pi writer would
synthesize readable content from tool results, but the original canonical
message retained empty content. Read-back verification then compared the
empty original against the synthesized readback and failed.

Fix (3 parts):
1. Pipeline (step 7b): normalize tool-only messages before writing by
   synthesizing text content ("[Tool: name]", "[Tool Output] ...",
   "[Tool Error] ...") into the canonical message itself.
2. Pi writer: stop emitting toolCall blocks alongside text blocks. Pi's
   reader extracts text from both block types, so emitting both would
   double up the content on read-back. A single text block is sufficient.
3. Regression test + fixture: codex_exec_tool_results.jsonl fixture with
   function_call and function_call_output events, verified roundtrip.

Fixes #9

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
